evidence: Why1: Two branches (security #337, gitignore #340) modified overlapping files with incompatible changes
Why2: PR #337 added an unrelated .gitignore !negate pattern that commits a binary video file, plus outdated dev/ ignore rule
Why3: PR #337 was created from main before PR #340 existed; both diverged with conflicting approaches to dev/ directory and video files
Why4: No package-lock.json regeneration in #337 after bumping vitest ^3.2.4 -> ^4.1.8
Why5: No cross-branch merge preview was performed before submitting

Root cause: PR #337 contains unnecessary .gitignore changes (negate pattern for binary video, stale dev/ rule), a committed binary video file, and unregenerated package-lock.json - all creating substantive merge issues when combined with #340
Accepted vitest ^4.1.8 bump from #337 in frontend/package.json
Removed binary video file from git tracking: git rm --cached backend/data/videos/Lavar-03gAoNcPO1g.mp4
package-lock.json already contained vitest 4.1.8 resolution from merge - no regeneration needed
cd frontend && npm test -> 14 test files passed, 182 tests passed (vitest 4.1.8)
cd frontend && npm run type-check -> tsc --noEmit (0 errors)
cd frontend && npm run lint -> eslint . --ext ts,tsx (0 errors)
